status.next_allowed_action: Implement the current task; after verified completion, advance to the next task or phase automatically

decision_log.reason: The work spans project dependency migration, backend event protocol, frontend state model, UI components, browser QA, and docs.

decision_log.decision: Upgrade project-internal pi-agent dependency instead of only wrapping old start/end events
decision_log.reason: The user explicitly requested alignment with newer omp event behavior and rejected MVP thinking.

decision_log.decision: Place the live tool runway inside the current assistant reply before body text
decision_log.reason: The user selected visual direction A.

decision_log.decision: Run an isolated Node probe before formal pi-agent dependency migration
decision_log.reason: The target @oh-my-pi package may be Bun-oriented or a deeper runtime migration rather than a direct dependency bump.

decision_log.decision: Use a schema-versioned shared Tool Status contract with backend/frontend fixture reuse
decision_log.reason: The backend and frontend must not silently drift on streamed event fields.

decision_log.decision: Treat stop, disconnect, and abort as first-class cancellation paths
decision_log.reason: The UI must not show a fake running state after the user stops a response, and the backend must clean up current work.

decision_log.decision: Coalesce rapid tool updates and cap long history summaries
decision_log.reason: OMP-like quality requires smooth rendering during long tool runs, not just raw event display.

decision_log.decision: Add request ownership, ordered writing, prompt concurrency guard, backend redaction, best-effort legacy history, and deterministic fake event QA
decision_log.reason: Codex outside voice identified these as the main ways the dynamic runway could look right but behave unreliably.

phases.goal: Migrate and verify project-internal pi-agent dependency capability

acceptance.check: Project dependency target and package-name decision recorded in decision_log
Isolated Node probe proves the target pi-agent SDK can be imported, can create a minimal session, and exposes usable tool start/update/end event fields before project dependency migration begins

Dependency reconnaissance treats the target package as a possible runtime migration and verifies SessionManager, auth/model config, ResourceLoader, extensions, message types, artifacts, and abort support
Isolated probe runs in a temporary directory with recorded command, package version, Node version, and reproducible evidence without modifying project package files or node_modules

tasks.title: Dependency/API reconnaissance and isolated Node probe

phases.goal: Expose stable backend tool status events

acceptance.check: Stable tool status adapter covers start, update, end, failure, missing args, and common tool formatting
Backend and frontend tests share contract event fixtures so SSE event fields cannot drift silently
Client abort or explicit stop calls the session abort path where supported and emits cancelled tool status endings for active tools
Stable event contract is schemaVersioned and every prompt event includes runId, messageId, and monotonic seq
/api/prompt uses a single ordered writer and same-session concurrent prompts are rejected or queued without mixed events
Backend redacts private local paths and long sensitive command targets before emitting tool status events or persisted summaries
node --import tsx --test workbench/server/src/tool-status-events.test.ts exits 0
npm run typecheck --workspace=@llm-wiki-agent/server exits 0
npm run typecheck exits 0

tasks.title: Connect /api/prompt SSE to stable tool status events
Preserve history display through generated summaries

phases.goal: Create frontend tool status model and stream integration

acceptance.check: Tool status reducer tests cover switching, missing args, failed/cancelled tools, parallel tools, and truncation
Reducer keys events by runId/messageId and ignores late, duplicate, or unknown-run events
Rapid tool update events are coalesced or throttled, and long tool histories are capped with a remaining-count summary
Streaming UI exposes a stop action and clears or cancels the live runway instead of leaving a stale running state

ToolHistorySummary defaults folded, groups tool types, and expands full detail
ChatPanel no longer renders long live .msg-tools streams during complex tasks
